summary.intent: Finish the ArsProvider adapter deliverables for both Leptos (#347) and Dioxus (#477) by adding integration test files, exposing ArsProvider in both preludes, and filling spec §28/§29 gaps — the component implementations already existed but the integration-tier surface was never completed.
summary.outcome: All deliverables implemented and committed: 4 new integration test files (22 tests passing), use_direction() hook added to both adapters (spec §22 gap), preludes updated symmetrically, both adapter specs updated, widget examples simplified to use prelude imports, cargo xci-fast all-green, and a PR opened closing both #347 and #477.

repo: Both ArsProvider components were fully implemented (ars-leptos/src/provider.rs:169-254, ars-dioxus/src/provider.rs:28-239) but had no adapter-tier integration tests and were not exposed in the preludes — issue #477 was closed prematurely as 'superseded by #193' without the discrete adapter deliverable being finished.
The use_direction() hook was required by Leptos spec §22 but was missing from both adapter implementations; use_style_strategy() existed in ars-dioxus but was undocumented in the Dioxus spec §22.

Dioxus --all-features activates the 'desktop' feature which switches the default platform from NullPlatform to DesktopPlatform, making SSR tests for dioxus_platform default feature-sensitive; tests must use default_dioxus_platform() dynamically rather than hardcoding a type.

All 6 widget examples were importing ArsProvider from the crate root (ars_*::{ArsProvider, prelude::t}) instead of the prelude because it wasn't re-exported there — a direct validator that the prelude additions were needed.
Integration test files in tests/ cannot access pub(crate) probe helpers defined in src/ — each test file must define its own local Probe/probe component.
cargo xci-fast is the workspace gate (~5 min) covering fmt, check, clippy, unit/integration, spec-compile-snippets, error-variant-coverage, and snapshot-count.

The project uses a post-implementation-audit skill with three phases: (1) spec/impl drift, (2) ≥2 rounds of 'anything missing?', (3) llvm-cov coverage audit — the audit found 5 distinct gaps before going clean.
Leptos SSR oracle pattern: view!{}.to_html(). Dioxus SSR oracle pattern: VirtualDom::new(app) → vdom.rebuild_in_place() → dioxus_ssr::render(&vdom).

code.finding: ArsProvider Leptos component implementation — fully spec-compliant before this PR; the gap was only integration tests and prelude exposure.
ArsProvider Dioxus component implementation — same situation; also contains default_dioxus_platform() which resolves the platform based on enabled feature flags.
Dioxus SSR test for dioxus_platform default must call default_dioxus_platform() dynamically rather than asserting a concrete type, because the desktop feature changes which platform is returned.

workflow: leptosfmt can cause widespread reformatting of unrelated files when run broadly; changes must be scoped carefully and unintended reformats reverted before committing.
wasm-pack test --chrome --headless is the canonical wasm test runner; the xtask wasm recipes wrap it per-crate.
GitHub Projects board status updates require manual gh CLI calls to find the project item IDs and Status field option IDs — not automatic when reopening issues.
The audit skill's no-deferral rule means all findings during the post-implementation-audit must land in the same PR rather than being filed as follow-ups.
When dogfooding new hooks in integration tests (using the hook rather than reading context directly), this simultaneously validates the hook and provides integration-tier coverage.

friction: Issue #477 was closed without a PR or linked implementation commit, requiring it to be reopened and adding confusion about what 'superseded' actually meant.

cargo xci-fast --all-features broke the Dioxus SSR dioxus_platform test because the desktop feature silently changes the default platform return value — required a non-obvious fix to call default_dioxus_platform() dynamically.
leptosfmt reformatted dozens of unrelated files in categories/ subdirectories, requiring manual git revert to keep the diff scoped to the actual changes.

use_direction() was listed as 'required' in Leptos spec §22 but was absent from both adapter implementations — a gap that only surfaced during the post-implementation audit, not during initial planning.

pub(crate) test helpers (ProviderProbe, ConfiguredProviderProbe) in inline tests are not accessible from tests/ integration files, requiring duplicate local probe component definitions in each test file.
open_items: Codex review loop (waiting-for-codex-review skill) after PR push — the transcript ends at commit/push without confirming the review loop was invoked.
Dioxus wasm tests (ars_provider_wasm.rs) were written and compiled but the transcript does not confirm they were run via wasm-pack in headless Chrome before the commit.

ArsContext is explicitly kept out of both preludes (by design) — advanced users must reach it via ars_leptos::ArsContext / ars_dioxus::ArsContext; this decision should be documented in the PR description to prevent reviewer questions.
